title: Intermediate
outcome: Speak in connected paragraphs, manage work and social conversations, and use practical grammar with more control.

chapters.title: Opinions, Ideas, and Discussion
chapters.summary: Move into real discussion language: opinions, reasons, examples, comparison, and conditional thinking.
contexts: a debate on a daily-life topic
lexicon: a change that happens over time
a set of reasons for a position
a structured discussion with different views
a personal way of seeing an issue
lessons: state a view without sounding unsure
show why one option is stronger
trend language and present continuous for change
Take Your Turn in a Discussion
enter and leave a conversation smoothly
build and defend a simple argument

chapters.title: Work Communication and Growth
chapters.summary: Upgrade workplace language for updates, meetings, feedback, negotiation, and career growth.
contexts: responding to a client or manager
lexicon: another message after the first one
a message that helps someone remember
a person or company receiving service
lessons: sound polite and direct in writing and speech
keep work moving without sounding rude
explain a problem and suggest a fix
push for a better result politely
handle client concerns with calm language
explain a useful idea with structure

chapters.title: Social Fluency and Real Interaction
chapters.summary: Sound more natural in friendships, group talk, storytelling, emotional conversations, and awkward moments.
lexicon: something said to make people laugh
words used to say sorry formally
the emotional feeling of a person or moment
an account of something that happened
lessons: sound warmer in first social moments
join, hold, and leave group talk smoothly
Talk About Movies, Books, and Shows
talk through tension or appreciation respectfully
stay fluent in longer social talk

chapters.title: Advanced Grammar in Use
chapters.summary: Use intermediate grammar in real speech instead of memorizing disconnected rules.
contexts: speaking more precisely in work or life
lexicon: a word or clause that adds more detail
a form where the action matters more than the doer
spoken again after another person said it
lessons: add extra information without breaking flow
focus on action and result when needed
say what someone else said naturally
show actions that started earlier and continue now
show the order of past events
modals of advice, obligation, and certainty
choose the right verb pattern after another verb
apply advanced grammar without sounding forced

chapters.title: Media, Society, and Problem Solving
chapters.summary: Use English to discuss current life topics, systems, customer issues, and public questions.
lexicon: the title of a news story
an online or physical place where activity happens
a statement that something is wrong
the steps in how something works
the strong effect of an action
lessons: share the key point of new information
explain personal or public health choices
describe how something works step by step
combine problem analysis and solution talk
